channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: US Marine from California identified as one of 4 people killed in Philippines plane crash
item.description: CAMP PENDLETON, Calif. (AP) — A U.S. Marine from California was identified by military officials Sunday as one of four people killed when a plane contracted by the U.S. military crashed in a rice field in the southern Philippines. Sgt. Jacob M.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Musk calls for impeachment of judge who blocked DOGE access at Treasury
item.description: The order explicitly prohibits special government employees and those detailed from outside the department from getting access to the systems.
item.dc:creator: Ian Swanson, NewsNation

item.title: UN humanitarian chief says Gaza ceasefire has averted famine but any truce collapse brings danger
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Famine has been mostly averted in Gaza as a surge of aid enters the territory during a fragile ceasefire, the United Nations humanitarian chief said Sunday. But he warned the threat could return quickly if the truce collapses.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Truck passenger shoots fireworks at car in alleged road rage incident caught on video
item.description: A pickup truck driver and passenger are wanted for questioning in Washington state after shooting fireworks at another driver in an “apparent road rage incident," authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How Republican skeptics in the Senate got to 'yes' on RFK Jr. and Gabbard
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republican skepticism in the Senate of President Donald Trump’sCabinet nominees has been worn down, putting his unconventional choices for some of the most powerful positions in the federal government on the verge of confirmation. Floor votes are expected this week on Robert F.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Sam Nujoma, Namibia's fiery freedom fighter and first president, dies aged 95
item.description: OSHAKATI, Namibia (AP) — Sam Nujoma, the fiery, white-bearded freedom fighter who led Namibia to independence from apartheid South Africa in 1990 and served as its first president for 15 years, coming to be known as the father of his nation, has died. He was 95.
item.dc:creator: ELIASER NDEYANALE, FARAI MUTSAKA and GERALD IMRAY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Kosovo votes for new parliament as foreign aid dwindles and talks with Serbia are stalled
item.description: PRISTINA, Kosovo (AP) — Kosovars cast their votes Sunday in a parliamentary election considered a key test for Prime Minister Albin Kurti as talks on normalizing ties with rival Serbia remain stalled and foreign funding for one of Europe's poorest countries in question.
item.dc:creator: LLAZAR SEMINI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Transgender, vegan 'Zizian' cult linked to Vermont border agent killing dependent on zapping human emotions
item.description: The killing of US Border Patrol agent David Maland in Vermont near the Canadian border appears to be linked to a transgendered cult on the West Coast.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: As female representation hits new highs among states, constitutions still assume officials are male
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — South Dakota’s constitution refers to the governor as a “he.” But for the past six years, the governor was a “she” — Kristi Noem, the first female to hold the position there. The conservative Republican pushed a constitutional amendment to change the gendered language.
item.dc:creator: SEJAL GOVINDARAO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: House Republicans mull taxes on scholarships and changes to student loan programs
item.description: As Republicans in Congress look for ways to slash spending, some legislators are floating new taxes on college scholarships, an end to student loan repayment plans and a big hike in taxes on university endowments.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne Mumphrey, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Hawaii is the rainbow capital of the world. Here's what that means
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — Hawaii's regular sunshine, short rain showers and clean air come together to create some of the planet's best conditions for viewing rainbows. They're such a frequent sight that a University of Hawaii professor calls the state the “rainbow capital of the world.
item.dc:creator: AUDREY McAVOY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Musk says he has no plans to buy TikTok
item.description: Despite ByteDance stating that they are not planning to sell, some investors have shown interest in buying the company.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Here's what we know about a commuter plane crash in Alaska that killed 10 people
item.description: JUNEAU, Alaska (AP) — Authorities are working to recover the wreckage of a plane crash in western Alaska that killed 10 people, while investigators are trying to determine what caused the small commuter aircraft to go down in the icy Bering Sea.
item.dc:creator: BECKY BOHRER, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Anthony Davis leaves his Dallas debut with an injury after seismic Luka Doncic trade
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — Anthony Davis left in the third quarter with a lower-body injury and didn't return in his debut with the Dallas Mavericks on Saturday after the seismic trade that sent Luka Doncic to the Los Angeles Lakers.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Philadelphia radio station bans Taylor Swift music ahead of Super Bowl
item.description: A release from the station claims that 94.5 is "pressing pause on all things Taylor Swift." The announcement came after a Swift fan and a Philly advertising agency spent the week trolling each other with ads in KC and Philly.
item.dc:creator: Dillon Seckington, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Recall for broccoli sold at Walmart in 20 states upgraded to highest risk level: FDA
item.description: The Food and Drug Administration has upgraded its classification of a broccoli recall initially reported in late December, labeling it as “Class I” — the agency’s most serious recall category.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KTLA 5

item.title: Missing Alaska flight found with no survivors: What to know
item.description: The plane, operated by Bering Air, had been traveling from Unalakleet to Nome when it vanished on Thursday, only to be found the next day.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, KTLA 5

item.title: Nelly delivers hits at 'Homecoming' Super Bowl week concert in historic New Orleans restaurant
item.description: Nelly brought his signature St. Louis swagger to a New Orleans institution, turning a nearly seven-decade-old restaurant into a Super Bowl week party.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Landrum Jr, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Tips to easily cancel your subscriptions
item.description: The FTC says that it received about 70 consumer complaints a day in 2024 about recurring subscriptions, up from 42 per day in 2021.
item.dc:creator: Kimberly Palmer, NewsNation

item.title: Longtime NFL player and coach Dick Jauron dies at 74
item.description: Longtime NFL player and coach Dick Jauron, who led the Chicago Bears to the playoffs and was voted AP coach of the year in 2001, died Saturday. He was 74.
item.dc:creator: Dave Skretta, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Family files lawsuit after mother, son die from food poisoning while on vacation
item.description: A grieving family filed a lawsuit after a mother and son died from food poisoning while vacationing at a Dominican Republic resort.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Unger, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Democratic AGs sue over DOGE access to Treasury payment systems
item.description: “As the richest man in the world, Elon Musk is not used to being told ‘no,’ but in our country, no one is above the law,” James said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KTLA 5

item.title: Federal judge blocks Elon Musk's DOGE from accessing sensitive US Treasury Department material
item.description: A federal judge early Saturday blocked Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency from accessing Treasury Department records that contain sensitive personal data such as Social Security and bank account numbers for millions of Americans. U.S. District Judge Paul A.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: T-Swift fan, Philly agency trolling each other ahead of Super Bowl LIX
item.description: A Taylor Swift fan from Boston and a Philadelphia-based advertising agency have spent the week leading up to Super Bowl LIX trolling each other with billboards in KC and Philly.
item.dc:creator: Dillon Seckington, KRON4

item.title: Trump ending Kennedy Center board members' terms, naming himself chair in place of Rubenstein
item.description: President Trump on Friday said he would end the terms of multiple members of the Kennedy Center board and make himself chair of a new board, putting himself at the helm of the Washington, D.C., cultural institution.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: NFL has no plans to end DEI practices, commissioner says
item.description: The NFL is committed to sticking to its DEI policies despite a change in its Super Bowl on-field messaging campaign
item.dc:creator: Libbey Dean, NewsNation

item.title: Starbucks offering free coffee Monday after the Super Bowl: How to get yours
item.description: Starbucks is offering a free pick-me-up on Monday for football fans who might be staying up late celebrating (or fuming over) the results of the Super Bowl.
item.dc:creator: Jane Alvarez-Wertz, Fox 5

item.title: Members of Congress denied access to Department of Education
item.description: Multiple members of Congress were denied entry to the Department of Education on Friday, according to videos and social media posts from lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Expect Super Bowl victory celebrations to look different after last year's deadly shooting
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Mo. (AP) — Expect more security — and nerves — at this year's Super Bowl victory celebration regardless of who wins Sunday’s matchup in New Orleans between the Kansas City Chiefs and Philadelphia Eagles.
item.dc:creator: HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH and MARYCLAIRE DALE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Fetterman says he'll vote against RFK Jr., Gabbard nominations
item.description: "I have voted against moving forward to the confirmation of Ms. Gabbard and Mr. Kennedy, and will be voting NO on their confirmations.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Arab, Muslim Americans blast Trump's 'grotesque' Gaza proposal
item.description: Arab American and Muslim leaders are blasting President Trump’s proposal to take over Gaza, warning that it’s antithetical to their beliefs and amounts to ethnic cleansing.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, NewsNation

item.title: Efforts to put carbon dioxide underground face less support in Trump's second term
item.description: U.S. efforts to put carbon dioxide from power plants and industrial facilities underground where they won't contribute to global warming could see less federal support under President Donald Trump although demand for the technology remains high.
item.dc:creator: Mead Gruver, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Louisiana woman arrested following death of Kansas City reporter covering Super Bowl LIX
item.description: A Louisiana woman has been arrested after she was allegedly caught using the reporter's credit cards in the New Orleans area.
item.dc:creator: Raeven Poole, KRON4

item.title: Slidell woman arrested in death investigation of Kansas City reporter
item.description: Louisiana law enforcement continue to investigate that death of a Kansas City reporter visiting New Orleans for Super Bowl coverage.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Hamilton, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Dog Show 101: What's what at the Westminster Kennel Club
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Westminster Kennel Club dog show is back for a 149th year — and back at New York's Madison Square Garden for the first time since early 2020.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: DOJ appeals block of birthright citizenship executive order
item.description: It's the first Justice Department appeal of a lawsuit challenging major Trump administration actions.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, ABC 4

item.title: Trump's Gaza plan shocks the world but finds support in Israel
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — President Donald Trump’s plan to seek U.S. ownership of the Gaza Strip and move out its population infuriated the Arab world. It stunned American allies and other global powers and even flummoxed members of Trump’s own party. The reaction in Israel was starkly different.
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, ABC 4
